---
title: Getting started with Git
description: An introductory course to version control with &nbsp;[![](img/logo_git.png){width="1.3em"}](https://git-scm.com/)
title-block-banner: true
---

:::{.topdef}

A version control system allows you to track changes to your files and save important versions of your projects that you can revisit or revert to later. It is a critical aspect to any serious workflow while prototyping code, building a website, developing software, or writing documents such as a thesis or papers in markdown, Typst, or LaTeX.

[Git](https://git-scm.com/)—a free and open source software—has become the standard tool for version control.

In this hands-on course, you will learn how Git works and how to get started putting your projects under version control.

:::

<br>
[[Start course ➤](intro_git.qmd)]{.topinline}

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LearningResource",
  "name": "Getting started with Git",
  "description": "A version control system allows you to track changes to your files and save important versions of your projects that you can revisit or revert to later. It is a critical aspect to any serious workflow while prototyping code, building a website, developing software, or writing documents such as a thesis or papers in markdown, Typst, or LaTeX.\n\nGit—a free and open source software—has become the standard tool for version control.\n\nIn this hands-on course, you will learn how Git works and how to get started putting your projects under version control.",
  "url": "https://mint.westdri.ca/git/top_intro",
  "learningResourceType": "course",
  "inLanguage": "en",
  "license": "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/",
  "provider": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SFU Research Computing Group"
  },
  "keywords": "Git, version-control",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Marie-Hélène Burle"
  }
}
</script>
